title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Wyoming
description: Voters in Wyoming will elect one U.S. senator in 2026 for the state’s Class II Senate seat. The race is notable because incumbent Cynthia Lummis is not seeking re-election, making it an open-seat contest in a state that has been strongly Republican in recent federal elections. Ballotpedia rates the general election as solid/safe Republican, and the main contrast so far is within the Republican primary between a sitting member of Congress and a challenger emphasizing term limits and outsider status.
polling_note: No public polling found for this race as of 2026-04-01.

candidates.name: Harriet Hageman
candidates.summary: Harriet Hageman is a Republican member of the U.S. House from Wyoming who is running for the state’s open U.S. Senate seat in 2026. Her campaign emphasizes opposition to federal overreach, protection of private property and water rights, and conservative governance. She previously worked as a lawyer and served in the Wyoming attorney general’s office before entering Congress.

summary_sources.title: HAGEMAN, Harriet | US House of Representatives: History, Art & Archives

career_history.description: Clerked for a federal appellate judge.

candidates.name: Jimmy Skovgard
candidates.summary: Jimmy Skovgard is a Republican challenger in Wyoming’s 2026 U.S. Senate race. His campaign presents him as an outsider focused on term limits, financial transparency, limited government, and a more citizen-driven style of representation. He says he served more than a decade in the Wyoming Army National Guard and has also run his own business.

summary_sources.title: A second Wyomingite has announced a run for U.S. Senate
Man who grew up in Big Horn Co. files for Senate

career_history.title: Captain
career_history.description: Served for more than a decade and was honorably discharged as a captain.

career_history.title: Business owner
career_history.description: Campaign materials and reporting describe him as having run his own business.

candidates.name: James Byrd
candidates.summary: James Byrd is the Democratic candidate in Wyoming’s 2026 U.S. Senate race. His campaign says he is focused on energy, healthcare access, and public lands, and he has framed his candidacy around representing Wyoming interests in Washington. Byrd previously served in the Wyoming House of Representatives, giving him prior legislative experience in state government.
